oneSentenceSummary: Across 15,260 active payloads, recurring vulnerability regimes are driven most reliably by crowded traffic lanes and debris burden rather than orbit label alone; the clearest result is congested LEO traffic, while cross-orbit and stewardship-heavy patterns remain more qualified.

projectSummary.question: Can representation learning uncover recurring orbital vulnerability regimes beyond simple orbit labels?
projectSummary.answer: Across 15,260 active payloads, recurring vulnerability regimes are driven most reliably by crowded traffic lanes and debris burden rather than orbit label alone; the clearest result is congested LEO traffic, while cross-orbit and stewardship-heavy patterns remain more qualified.
projectSummary.modelValueAdd: The autoencoder adds a compact, better-separated view of the structure, but some of the same broad patterns are visible in simpler baselines.

sourceSummary.broadCoverage: Broad population coverage comes from the CelesTrak active SATCAT catalog.
sourceSummary.nasaScope: NASA-derived orbit windows are explicitly limited to the 8 curated story satellites and are not the source of broad population coverage.
sourceSummary.featureScope: Traffic, shell, and orbital geometry features are mostly direct or derived from public catalog data; mission and stewardship signals include heuristic proxies.

stronglySupported: Crowded LEO traffic is the clearest and most repeatable vulnerability pattern. The dominant LEO constellation regime appears in every seeded rerun, and a second crowded LEO regime appears in four of five.
Traffic density and debris-related signals are the most reliable drivers of fragility structure. The main crowded LEO and SSO patterns remain visible when stewardship proxy inputs are reduced.
Mission and operator context sharpens interpretation, but it is not the sole driver of the main regimes. The primary traffic-heavy patterns do not disappear when that metadata is reduced.
moderatelySupported: A smaller cross-orbit regime persists across most reruns and also shows up in simpler baselines, suggesting real mixed-regime structure beyond a single orbit label.
GEO-like assets form a calmer regime with weaker end-of-life and stewardship signals, but that regime is only moderately stable and depends more heavily on proxy-rich inputs.
tentative: Stewardship-heavy interpretations and some mission-specific regime labels remain sensitive to heuristic proxy features.
Outlier rankings are useful for spotting unusual satellites, but the top cases blend true orbital extremity with proxy-informed stewardship context.
modelValueAdd: The autoencoder gives the cleanest compressed view of the regime structure, with better separation than raw-feature clustering or PCA in its learned space.
Simpler baselines recover some of the same cross-orbit structure, so the neural model should be read as a summarization layer rather than the sole source of discovery.
Its main value is turning a 44-feature orbital context table into a compact regime map that the narrative shell can surface without becoming an analysis dashboard.

robustRegimes.label: Fragile LEO constellation traffic
robustRegimes.whyItMatters: Average score 63.69/100, weakest shared signal: congestion impact. This pattern is robust across reruns.

robustRegimes.label: Fragile SSO constellation traffic
robustRegimes.whyItMatters: Average score 66.52/100, weakest shared signal: debris exposure. This pattern is robust across reruns.

robustRegimes.label: Fragile LEO crowding pressure
robustRegimes.whyItMatters: Average score 61.29/100, weakest shared signal: congestion impact. This pattern is robust across reruns.

qualifiedRegimes.label: Working GEO stewardship strain
qualifiedRegimes.whyItMatters: Average score 79.63/100, weakest shared signal: sustainability. This pattern appears often, but not in every rerun.

qualifiedRegimes.label: Fragile Cross-orbit debris pressure
qualifiedRegimes.whyItMatters: Average score 61.19/100, weakest shared signal: debris exposure. This pattern is robust across reruns.

anomalySummary.headline: The outliers are dominated by unusual geometry and unusually weak local shell context, not by one single risk story.
anomalySummary.body: Top global outliers such as IBEX stand out because their orbital geometry or shell context looks unusual relative to peers. Those outliers are useful for inspection, but they should be read as prompts for closer review rather than as a standalone vulnerability ranking.

limitations: The cross-orbit regime appears in most reruns, but it is not unique to the autoencoder and should be framed as shared structure rather than purely neural discovery.
The calmer GEO stewardship interpretation is useful, but it is only moderately stable and more sensitive to proxy-heavy inputs than the crowded LEO regimes.
Proxy-heavy signals remain part of the model, so stewardship and mission-profile conclusions should be treated as informed interpretation rather than hard physical truth.
validationNote: Validated across 5 seeded reruns: the model settles around 5.2 regimes, the crowded LEO patterns remain the clearest result, and a cross-orbit regime appears in 80% of runs. That cross-orbit pattern is meaningful, but it is not unique to the neural embedding.
